observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following
courses of action:
a. The Municipal Treasurer
i. Prioritize the finalization of the
stratified schedule of presumptive
income level based on industry
classifications, business size,
location, and other relevant
operational indicators to approximate
the gross receipts of each business
classification for every tax period.
Once established, it may serve as a
standard reference in estimating gross
sales or receipts for businesses that
fail to provide sufficient documentation.
ii. Require all persons, who are
granted a permit to conduct an
activity or business and who are
liable to pay the business tax to
submit a certified photocopy of their
income tax returns (ITR) on or
before April 30 of each year and
collect any tax deficiency, if any,
including interest at the rate of ten
percent (10%) corresponding to the
two percent (2%) per month from
January to May. Otherwise, enforce
the corresponding penalty for non-
compliance therewith;
iii. Conduct a thorough investigation
into the underlying causes of the
decline in the number of business
permits and license renewals,
including potential cases of
unreported business closures and
enjoin business establishments to
settle local taxes, fees, and charges
prior to the formal retirement or
closure of their operations;
iv. Collect the tax deficiency from the
concerned business entities; and
v. Enforce other remedies to ensure
that the correct amount of fees and
taxes are collected.
b. The Business Permit and Licensing
Officer to ensure that applicants (i)
submit a complete set of all
documentary requirements in
securing permits and licenses prior to
the approval of the Local Chief
Executive; and (ii) declare the correct
amount of gross sales in their
application consistent with the
provisions of the Revenue Ordinance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that no additional cash advances
will be granted to any employee or officer unless all previous advances have been
fully liquidated and settled in accordance with existing regulations; and
b. ensure that (i) all accountable officers are properly bonded in accordance with
their actual functions; and (ii) their cash accountabilities do not exceed the maximum
amount covered by their approved bond.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer, being new to the position,
admitted that they had inadvertently overlooked some regulations on the grant and
liquidation of cash advances. Nevertheless, she committed to correcting the noted
deficiencies moving forward.
observations.reason:
Oversight and inadvertent non-compliance by previous office holders; new
Treasurer commits to corrective action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to (a) ensure that appropriations for
20% Development Fund comply with the provision on non-inclusion of expenditure
items that are not allowed to be charged against the 20% DF; (b) instruct the Local
Development Council and establish a formal procedure for project identification to
strengthen the planning process by substantiating project proposals with detailed
specifications and cost estimates; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to verify all
supporting documents as to completeness and see to it that the same are attached to the
disbursement vouchers before effecting payments thereof.
observations.managementAction:
The newly appointed Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator commented that management has started updating the Annual Investment
Program (AIP) for 20% Development Fund and assured the Audit Team that they will
comply with the provision of the JMC and related issuances on the preparation of the
AIP.
observations.reason:
While updating of AIP initiated, issues remain: prior inappropriate line items,
non-implementation of some PPAs, and deficient documentation of labor payrolls.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to instruct the (i) concerned
department heads to secure the necessary Memoranda of Agreement or Terms of
Agreement for all funds received from source agencies to ensure clear accountability and
prevent any potential misinterpretation or misuse of the transferred funds; and (b) focal
person of the Social Pension to post the final list of beneficiaries to promote
transparency and accountability in the selection and distribution process.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and/or Treasurer ensure
compliance with the posting and reportorial requirements of each trust fund not only to
maintain transparency and accountability in the management of financial resources
sourced externally but more importantly to secure future funding opportunities.
observations.managementAction:
The Secretary of the Sangguniang Bayan assured that copy of the notarized
Memoranda of Agreement will be secured from the source agency. On the other hand,
 the focal person for the distribution of the Social Pension has committed to complying
with the required posting of final list of beneficiaries moving forward.
observations.reason:
MOAs for certain fund transfers not available on file; posting of SPISC
beneficiary list was not done but committed to be done going forward; reportorial
requirements for some trust-funded projects not complied with.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the BAC
Secretariat to consolidate the Annual Procurement Plan taking into account the inclusion
of procurements considered crucial to the efficient discharge of governmental
functions and ensure that the same has been approved by the proper authority.
We further recommended that Management consolidate all procurement
requirements to enable the Municipality to maximize cost-efficiency through bulk
purchases.
observations.managementAction:
Although the APP was prepared and signed by the General Services Officer
instead of the HoPE or second-ranking official designated by the HoPE to act on his
behalf, management submitted a copy of the APP. Documentation of procurement
proceedings has improved. However, APP remained limited to common-use supplies
and did not include all crucial procurements; multiple small procurements persisted.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and the management agreed to: (a)
initiate strict control measures in reviewing the supporting documents of the payments of
salaries to job order personnel; and (b) require the 119 job order personnel to appear at the
COA office for their evaluation and verification.
observations.managementAction:
The HRMO Head, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
acknowledged the audit team's findings, citing heavy workloads as a reason for the lack
of thorough reviews. They assured that proper reviews would be conducted moving
forward. The HRMO committed to instruct the unverified JO personnel to report for
validation.
observations.reason:
Many DTRs and payroll supporting documents remained unsigned or
incomplete; 119 JO personnel remained unverified as of audit despite commitments to
validate.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) in his capacity as Chairman of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), consider the
amendment of the composition or reconstitution of the GFPS-TWG to include all other
mandatory members thereof;
b) direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, together with the GAD
Focal Person and TWG, to exert effort in establishing a GAD database that will serve as
basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation pending the
receipt of the result of the Community-Based Monitoring System; and
c) instruct the GAD FPS to (i) submit the documentary requirements supporting the
GAD Accomplishment Report; (ii) undergo capacity-building programs; and (iii)
consistently coordinate with the DILG for any changes in the GAD Plan and Budget and
update the portal as required.
observations.managementAction:
The GFPS focal person committed to ensuring that the foregoing
recommendations will be properly addressed. On the other hand, the MPDC asserted
that they currently relied on the data requested from the provincial government pending
the receipt of the result of the CBMS.
observations.reason:
Composition of GFPS ExeCom and TWG still lacks required members; GAD
AR lacked supporting documents; GAD database not yet established pending CBMS
results; GPB adjustments not uploaded; GFPS members did not attend planned
trainings.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed, thru the MENRO and Solid
Waste Management Board, to ensure that the submitted 10-year Solid Waste
Management Plan will be presented to and approved by the NSWMC.
observations.managementAction:
The designated MENRO disclosed that a new 10-year SWMP has been
prepared by the municipality and was submitted for review DENR-EMB ROIII on
December 23, 2024. The municipality continued its partnership with Geocycle Holcim
Philippines in collecting residual wastes with diversion potential; 1,620.84 tons diverted
in CY 2024.
observations.reason:
10-year SWMP submitted for review but not yet approved by NSWMC; some
solid waste initiatives (e.g., diversion with Holcim) implemented.

observations.recommendation:
For past due accounts based on the GSIS-EBCS, we reiterated and the concerned
departments agreed to continuously coordinate and follow up with the GSIS until all
discrepancies between the Municipality's records and those of the GSIS are fully
resolved.
observations.managementAction:
Conferred with the GSIS to reconcile discrepancies between the records of the
Municipality and the GSIS. Some reconciliation actions undertaken; balance decreased
from ₱907,506.36 in 2023 to ₱498,371.66 in 2024. Municipal Treasurer to continue
coordination; budget appropriation for government shares in arrears not fully done.
observations.reason:
Conferred with GSIS and partly reconciled; some prior-period unpaid premiums
remain; appropriation for arrears not fully arranged.

observations.recommendation:
1.23 We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to
instruct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory and
submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment with complete description to the office of the Audit Team not
later than January 31 of each year;
b. General Service Officer (GSO) to (i) maintain Property Cards for each
type of property in coordination with the Office of the Municipal
Accountant; (ii) issue the corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for
Equipment (ARE) for all properties with complete data to fix the
accountability of the end-user over the property; (iii) maintain Local Road
Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card; and
(iv) secure all documents or deed of donation from the donor of donated
properties and furnish the Accounting a copy for proper recognition of the
same in the books of accounts; otherwise, refer the matter to the Appraisal
Committee for the determination of the appraised value of all donated
properties;
c. Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC), Construction-in-Progress Ledger
Cards (CIPLC) and Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC); and (ii)
recognize donated properties in the books of accounts;
d. Municipal Engineering Office to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the GSO the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects as basis of the Municipal Accountant to
record the same in the books of accounts; and
e. Municipal Assessor to immediately survey the landholdings declared
under the name of the Municipality and assist the General Services Officer
to undertake the necessary actions for the registration and titling thereof.

1.24 We further recommended that management coordinate with the DPWH
Pampanga 1st District Engineering Office to obtain the necessary documents of
transfer and henceforth recognize all the completed infrastructure projects in the
Municipality’s books.

2.25 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:
a. Municipal Engineer, being the building official, to inform all applicants of
building permits of their responsibilities after the completion of the
structure, which include, among others, the filing of a sworn declaration
of the true value of the structure. Likewise, inspect properties applied for
building permits that have not yet been applied for an occupancy permit
and compel the owner to perform their duties mandated by the law;
b. Municipal Assessor to verify the status of undeclared property and
henceforth declare the same in the name of the defaulting owner, if known,
or against an unknown owner, as the case may be, and assess the property
for taxation in accordance with laws, rules and regulations;
c. Municipal Treasurer to (i) regularly prepare and issue the necessary notice
of delinquency and demand letters to delinquent taxpayers; and (ii) post
the notice of the delinquency at the main entrance hall and in a publicly
accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay of the municipality
where the property is located; and
d. Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to recognize the
correcting entries on the understated property tax receivable accounts.

3.38 We reiterated the following courses of action and management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer
i. Prioritize the finalization of the stratified schedule of presumptive
income level based on industry classifications, business size, location,
and other relevant operational indicators to approximate the gross
receipts of each business classification for every tax period. Once
established, it may serve as a standard reference in estimating gross
sales or receipts for businesses that fail to provide sufficient
documentation.
ii. Require all persons, who are granted a permit to conduct an activity or
business and who are liable, to pay the business tax to submit a certified
photocopy of their income tax returns (ITR) on or before April 30 of
each year and collect any tax deficiency, if any, including interest at the
rate of ten percent (10%) corresponding to the two percent (2%) per
month from January to May. Otherwise, enforce the corresponding
penalty for non-compliance therewith;
iii. Conduct a thorough investigation into the underlying causes of the
decline in the number of business permits and license renewals,
including potential cases of unreported business closures and enjoin
business establishments to settle local taxes, fees, and charges prior to
the formal retirement or closure of their operations;
iv. Collect the tax deficiency from the concerned business entities; and
v. Enforce other remedies to ensure that the correct amount of fees and
taxes are collected.
b. The Business Permit and Licensing Officer ensure that applicants (i)
submit a complete set of all documentary requirements in securing
permits and licenses prior to the approval of the Local Chief Executive;
and (ii) declare the correct amount of gross sales in their application
consistent with the provisions of the Revenue Ordinance.

4.8 We recommended and management agreed to (a) advise the Local Finance
Committee and other officials concerned to regularly monitor cash position and cease
forthwith the accrual of any cash overdraft; b) adopt austerity measures and incur
expenditures only for projects, programs, and activities that are highly necessary and
cannot be dispensed with; and (c) ensure that trust liabilities are used solely for their
intended purpose.

5.33 We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that no additional cash
advances will be granted to any employee or officer unless all previous
advances have been fully liquidated and settled in accordance with existing
regulations; and
b. ensure that (i) all accountable officers are properly bonded in accordance
with their actual functions; and (ii) their cash accountabilities do not
exceed the maximum amount covered by their approved bond.

6.18 We recommended and management agreed to (a) ensure that appropriations
for 20% Development Fund comply with the provision on non-inclusion of
expenditure items that are not allowed to be charged against the 20% DF; (b) instruct
the Local Development Council and establish a formal procedure for project
identification to strengthen the planning process by substantiating project proposals
with detailed specifications and cost estimates; and (c) require the Municipal
Accountant to verify all supporting documents as to completeness and see to it that
the same are attached to the disbursement vouchers before effecting payments
thereof.

7.31 We recommended and management agreed to:
(a) prioritize the creation of the mandatory positions required by R.A. No.
10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 for the effective
and efficient discharge of the duties and responsibilities of the LDRRMO;
(b) instruct the LDRRM Officer to observe the guidelines in the preparation
of the LDRRMFIP pursuant to JMC No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No.
2012-002, ensuring that prior years’ unexpended balances and other
sources are included; and showing, among others, the allocation of funds
for every PPA, schedule of implementation, implementing office, expected
output, funding source and expense class;
(c) instruct the concerned personnel to prepare, maintain and submit the
required registries and other reportorial requirements; and
(d) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) properly check the accuracy and
completeness of all supporting documents before payment; and (ii) include
the appropriate disclosure to the Financial Statements to inform
stakeholders of the receipt, distribution, and status of relief in compliance
with Section 2, Item VII of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15,
2014.

8.23 We recommended and the management agreed to instruct the (i) concerned
department heads to secure the necessary Memoranda of Agreement or Terms of
Agreement for all funds received from source agencies to ensure clear accountability and
prevent any potential misinterpretation or misuse of the transferred funds; and (b) focal
person of the Social Pension to post the final list of beneficiaries to promote transparency
and accountability in the selection and distribution process.

8.24 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and/or Treasurer
ensure compliance with the posting and reportorial requirements of each trust fund not
only to maintain transparency and accountability in the management of financial
resources sourced externally but more importantly to secure future funding
opportunities.

9.11 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the BAC
Secretariat to consolidate the Annual Procurement Plan taking into account the
inclusion of procurements considered crucial to the efficient discharge of
governmental functions and ensure that the same has been approved by the proper
authority.

9.12 We further recommended that Management consolidate all procurement
requirements to enable the Municipality to maximize cost-efficiency through bulk
purchases.

10.12 We reiterated our recommendations and the management agreed to: (a)
initiate strict control measures in reviewing the supporting documents of the
payments of salaries to job order personnel; and (b) require the 119 job order
personnel to appear at the COA office for their evaluation and verification.

11.25 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) in his capacity as Chairman of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS),
consider the amendment of the composition or reconstitution of the GFPS-
TWG to include all other mandatory members thereof;
b) direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, together with the
GAD Focal Person and TWG, to exert effort in establishing a GAD
database that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning,
programming and policy formulation pending the receipt of the result of
the Community-Based Monitoring System; and
c) instruct the GAD FPS to (i) submit the documentary requirements
supporting the GAD Accomplishment Report; (ii) undergo capacity-
building programs; and (iii) consistently coordinate with the DILG for any
changes in the GAD Plan and Budget and update the portal as required.

12.13 We recommended and the management agreed, thru the MENRO and Solid
Waste Management Board, to ensure that the submitted 10-year Solid Waste
Management Plan will be presented to and approved by the NSWMC.

13.9 However, with regard to past due accounts reflected in the GSIS-eBCS, we
reiterated and the concerned departments agreed to continuously coordinate and
follow up with the GSIS until all discrepancies between the Municipality's records
and those of the GSIS are fully resolved.

14.6 We commended Management on proper withholding of premiums and loan
repayments of its personnel, in compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679 and
its IRR, and the prompt remittance thereof together with the government shares
within the prescribed period outlined in HDMF Circular No. 275. Nevertheless, we
recommended and the management agreed to continually observe the requirements
of both issuances.

15.10 We commended Management for the continuous compliance with existing
laws, rules, and regulations on proper deduction and remittance of PhilHealth
contributions of its employees and personnel pursuant to the provisions of R.A. No.
10606, as amended. Nevertheless, we recommended and management agreed to (i)
continue to observe the required withholding and remittance of PhilHealth
contributions for the benefit of its personnel; and (ii) confer with PhilHealth to
reconcile and clear the records of the municipality.

16.13 We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to (i) prepare subsidiary
ledgers to support the Due to BIR account balance stated in the Financial Statements;
and (ii) investigate other reasons for the negative balance.

16.14 Likewise, we henceforth recommended that management adhere to the
required remittance of withholding taxes within the reglementary period.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) advise the Local Finance
Committee and other officials concerned to regularly monitor cash position and cease
forthwith the accrual of any cash overdraft; b) adopt austerity measures and incur
expenditures only for projects, programs, and activities that are highly necessary and
cannot be dispensed with; and (c) ensure that trust liabilities are used solely for their
intended purpose.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer, being new to the
position, admitted that her office has faced
challenges in improving cash position and
management. However, for CY 2025, she
has started measures in order to eliminate the
cash overdraft and assured the audit Team
that this will not happen again in the
ensuing period.
observations.reason:
Newly appointed Treasurer initiated measures to improve cash position;
no further details provided.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to:
(a) prioritize the creation of the mandatory positions required by R.A. No. 10121 and
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 for the effective and efficient discharge of
the duties and responsibilities of the LDRRMO;
(b) instruct the LDRRM Officer to observe the guidelines in the preparation of the
LDRRMFIP pursuant to JMC No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002, ensuring that
prior years’ unexpended balances and other sources are included; and showing, among
others, the allocation of funds for every PPA, schedule of implementation, implementing
office, expected output, funding source and expense class;
(c) instruct the concerned personnel to prepare, maintain and submit the required
registries and other reportorial requirements; and
(d) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) properly check the accuracy and
completeness of all supporting documents before payment; and (ii) include the
appropriate disclosure to the Financial Statements to inform stakeholders of the receipt,
distribution, and status of relief in compliance with Section 2, Item VII of COA Circular
No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.
observations.managementAction:
Substantial actions taken on some items: LDRRM activities
implemented; however staffing gap persists as one mandatory position not filled. LDRRM
FIP prepared but incomplete as to prior years’ unexpended balances and expense class
breakdown. In-kind donations were received but required registries and disclosures not
fully prepared. Municipal Accountant to improve vetting of supporting documents.
observations.reason:
One of the three mandatory LDRRMO positions remained unfilled;
LDRRMFIP incomplete and registries/reportorial requirements not fully complied
with; inadequate documentary support for disbursements; in-kind donations not fully
recorded/disclosed.

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management on proper withholding of premiums and loan
repayments of its personnel, in compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679 and its
IRR, and the prompt remittance thereof together with the government shares within the
prescribed period outlined in HDMF Circular No. 275. We also recommended that the
Accounting Office continually observe the requirements of both issuances.
observations.managementAction:
Accounting Office continually observed the requirements of HDMF and
remitted premiums and loan amortizations within prescribed periods for CY 2024 as
verified by official receipts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to (a) determine the causes of the discrepancies between the accounting books and the cash books for subsequent appropriate action; and (b) conduct reconciliation of cash account records at least quarterly to identify the nature of differences and record the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The MTO continuously failed to reconcile its record with the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct LDRRMO, GSO and MAO to
 
a.	review and reconcile all records to track and trace all donations during the year and implement necessary recording and accounting procedures as provided under COA Circular 2014-002; and 

b.	ensure that all funds and properties, both internally funded and donated, are properly recorded and accounted for as prescribed.
observations.reason: Donated properties were not recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) immediately submit the 4th quarter financial document; and (b) exert more efforts to improve compliance with the regulations on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned improve compliance in furnishing the Audit Team with copies of contracts/ purchase orders and supporting documents and Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. This is designed to enable the audit team to perform its duties relating to post audit of the transactions, at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.
observations.reason: Copies of Contract and purchase orders together with their supporting documents relative to the procurement of goods and services were still not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with the mandatory documentation of procurement proceedings;
Municipal Accountant (i) coordinate with the BAC in the submission of the required documents to the Office of the Auditor for further evaluation; (ii) ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment to substantiate the regularity of claims; and (iii) prepare a note to disclosure in the financial statements on the status of the receipt and issuance of in-kind donation; and
Management install an effective and efficient internal control over the receipt and issuance of in-kind donations in conformity with COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.
Management Comment:	The management committed to install an effective and efficient internal control over the receipt and issuance of in-kind donations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: 
(a) BAC or its Secretariat and the Municipal Engineer to submit the required documents as presented above in accordance with RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; 
(b) BAC, Municipal Engineer and the Accountant, as appropriate, to submit written explanation on the noted deficiencies in the documentation of the said transactions; and 
(c) Supplier to submit a copy of the quality testing results performed on materials used.
observations.managementAction:
LGU will ensure the submission of all required documents in accordance with RA 9184 and COA Circular No 2012-001.

The LGU will Strictly comply with the documentary requirements.

Quality Testing Results performed on materials used will be submitted to the resident Auditor’s Office
observations.reason: The Municipality partially submitted deficient documents. Materials Testing for completed roads were submitted but not for other projects..

observations.managementAction: For the current year 2017, the MPDO, in coordination with the Office of the Budget Officer, is already complying with the budgeting and utilization guidelines set out in DILG-DBM Joint Memo Circ No. 2011-1.
observations.reason: PPAs were still not specified. Some projects are still considered soft projects instead of prescribed capital expenditures.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that 
(a) GAD focal person initiate the establishment and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and 
(b) GAD board to re-evaluate the achievement of objectives thru the full implementation of programmed GAD PPAs before augmenting the same to other PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator has started collating data for the establishment of the GAD Database from key departments of the municipality. The GAD Board re-evaluated the plans and programs and shall effect the necessary revisions on succeeding budget year.
observations.reason: Existing data were outdated.  Waiting for the updated data from PSA and other agencies.

observations.managementAction: An audience with the Division Superintendent is requested as of writing although the discussion with the Local School Board reiterated the need for a well balanced budget and all disbursements must have proper documentation complete with all the necessary supporting documents.
observations.reason: Decrease in disbursements classified as improper charges to the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer 
(a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay in the verification and post-audit activities; and
 (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and Treasurer’s Office deliberated and agreed to efficiently manage the recording of transactions for timely reporting. Bottlenecks on the flow of documents and other problem areas were identified and are to be addressed immediately.
observations.reason: Compliance with prescribed submission significantly improved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and reiterated that management require the Municipal Treasurer to pay all purchases through checks except for petty expenditures to avoid the possible misuse of government funds and to withhold the corresponding taxes due to the government; and in compliance with Section 93 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Management is exerting all necessary effort to do away with reimbursements. Although in some special cases, direct procurement/payment to suppliers is somehow unreasonable just like in the procurement of MHPTC/TESDA consumables and other needs.
observations.reason: Significant decrease in the number of reimbursements. However, the practice still exists.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that for future similar projects, the: 

Municipal Agriculturist coordinate with the DA in identifying the number of farmers who shall benefit from future similar project, the kind, amount and importance of agriculture products in the area; and

Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist, ensure that the requirements of RA No. 8435 are given careful consideration in the implementation of FMR projects which aims to prioritize the conveyance of foods and services, and that projects to be undertaken are located within the key production areas or marginal lands which link these areas to major markets, post-harvest facilities, and processing zones/industries.
observations.managementAction: The lists of farmers/beneficiaries were submitted to the Resident Auditor’s office on July 11, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, henceforth, ensure that the required plans and specifications and the detailed unit price analysis are prepared prior to implementation thereof.
observations.managementAction: LGU will ensure compliance to all documentary requirements in accordance with RA 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:

require the Contractor to cause the repair of the portion of the project found defective at no additional cost to the Municipality; and

henceforth, ensure compliance with quality control policies and minimum tests requirements to assure quality of materials and mixtures to avoid the occurrence of structural defects and deficiencies within the life expectancy of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Management will ensure that the defective portions of the said road, should they progress into damages, will be repaired and put to order since this project was implemented by administration due to limited funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer strictly comply with DPWH DO No. 11, series of 2014 on the design standards for FMR projects.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with DPWH DO No. 11, series of 2014 on the design standards for FMR projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer strictly comply with the provisions of DOLE DO No. 13 on occupational safety and health.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with the provisions of DOLE DO No. 13 on occupational safety and health.

The recommendation to organize Pakyaw Labor Groups for Pakyaw Contracts has now been mobilized based on existing provisions and regulations. This will allow the Municipality to have access to a labor pool for projects implemented through administration for easy future references.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Procurement Officer comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 on timely furnishing of copies of PO to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management will require the Procurement Officer, in similar future undertakings, to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009 – 001.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, for future projects, the Municipal/Project Engineer strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the posting of the required information on billboards to promote transparency in implementation of projects.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013 – 004 on the posting of relevant project information on billboards for transparency in government service.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer/Municipal Planning Officer submit the lacking documents;

Chairman of the BAC and Municipal Accountant submit a written explanation on the noted documentary deficiencies; and

Municipal Accountant require the Supplier to submit the Sales Invoice for the purchased construction materials.
observations.managementAction:
It is not the intention of the Municipality to circumvent the documentary requirements, although the limited funds allotted to this relatively small project was used solely for its construction. 
The technical specifications of the FMR project were based from the previous related projects so as not to spend on testing and other technical requirements. 
Anyway, this FMR is being used by light vehicles only which based from experience, the current specification can withstand such load. 
As per the other documentary requirements, the LGU will try its best to comply.
